heading: Consuls, vice consuls, and members of a diplomatic mission as defendant
label: 1351 Consuls, vice consuls, and members of a diplomatic mission as defendant
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., §§ 41(18), 371(8) (, §§ 24, par. 18, 256, par. 8, , 1160).Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 23136 Stat. 1093Words “civil action” were substituted for “suits,” and “all suits and proceedings” in view of Rule 2 of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure.Changes were made in phraseology.
References in TextSection 2 of the Diplomatic Relations Act, referred to in par. (2), is classified to , Foreign Relations and Intercourse.section 254a of Title 22

Amendments1978— substituted “Consuls, vice consuls, and members of a diplomatic mission as defendant” for “Consuls and vice consuls as defendants” in section catchline, designated existing provisions as introductory provision preceding par. (1), and in such introductory provision as so designated, substituted “civil actions and proceedings against—” for “actions and proceedings against consuls or vice consuls of foreign states”, and added pars. (1) and (2).Pub. L. 95–3931949—Act , substituted “of all actions and proceedings” for “of any civil action”.May 24, 1949

Effective Date of 1978 AmendmentAmendment by effective at end of ninety-day period beginning on , see , set out as an Effective Date note under , Foreign Relations and Intercourse.Pub. L. 95–393section 9 of Pub. L. 95–393section 254a of Title 22Sept. 30, 1978

children.text: consuls or vice consuls of foreign states; or
members of a mission or members of their families (as such terms are defined in section 2 of the Diplomatic Relations Act).
